name: Paragonimiasis (Lung Fluke)
description: A food-borne parasitic lung infection caused by the lung fluke. Contracted by eating raw, pickled, or undercooked freshwater crabs or crayfish containing infective metacercariae larvae. Simulates tuberculosis.

climateLinks.impact: Warmer waters expand populations of intermediate snail hosts and freshwater crabs, facilitating fluke transmission cycles.

prevention: Thoroughly cooking all freshwater crabs and crayfish
Avoiding contamination of water sources with feces or sputum
Safe food preparation practices in rural outposts
triageAdvisory: Clinical features: Chronic productive cough, rusty brown sputum, and chest pain mimicking tuberculosis. Confirm via sputum or stool microscopy for eggs. Treat with Praziquantel.
